2026-04-07T08:47:46.7182719Z Agent: 
2026-04-07T08:47:46.7183462Z Agent: Wait, `delete` IS a reserved JavaScript keyword. Using it as a property name in an interface is actually fine in JavaScript/TypeScript - you can have `obj.delete` or `interface { delete: fn }`. 
2026-04-07T08:47:46.7184278Z Agent: 
2026-04-07T08:47:46.7185558Z Agent: But I'm noticing this was flagged as an issue in PR #37954 for `DependencyOfRelationshipsOperations.delete` with the note about using `@clientName` in client.tsp to fix it. Even though `delete` works as a property name syntactically, it seems the SDK guidelines treat it as something that should be renamed to avoid confusion or potential issues.

to specify what pull request data needs to be retrieved from GitHub. \nPossible options: \n 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.\n 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.\n 3. get_status - Get combined commit status of a head commit in a pull request.\n 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.\n 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.\n 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method.\n 7. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. 2026-04-07T08:47:46.9439352Z ##[group]Run # Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts
2026-04-07T08:47:46.9439604Z [36;1m# Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts[0m
2026-04-07T08:47:46.9439745Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-04-07T08:47:46.9440205Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-04-07T08:47:46.9440540Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
2026-04-07T08:47:46.9440655Z [36;1mif command -v awf &> /dev/null; then[0m
2026-04-07T08:47:46.9440799Z [36;1m  awf logs summary | tee -a "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"[0m
2026-04-07T08:47:46.9440876Z [36;1melse[0m
2026-04-07T08:47:46.9441051Z [36;1m  echo 'AWF binary not installed, skipping firewall log summary'[0m
2026-04-07T08:47:46.9441124Z [36;1mfi[0m
